channel.title: Home on Ezra Natanael
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Ezra Natanael

item.title: LaTeX Crash Course
item.description: Introduction LaTeX is a typesetting system widely used for academic and scientific documents. Its popularity comes from its powerful control over document layout, especially for complex structures like mathematical formulas, tables, and references. In this crash course, we’ll explore the essentials of LaTeX, from basic setup to creating professional documents. Why Use LaTeX? LaTeX is ideal for anyone working on: Academic papers, theses, or dissertations Mathematical or scientific documents Professional presentations and reports Documents with precise formatting needs LaTeX shines with complex documents due to:

item.title: The Docker Cookbook
item.description: Introduction Improving scalability, consistency, and streamlining procedures are critical in today’s fast-paced development environment. Docker, a platform that enables developers to bundle apps and their dependencies into lightweight, portable containers, can help with this. Docker makes it easier to set up environments and deploy apps across several computers, whether you’re working on a personal project or managing an advanced enterprise program. Software may be executed in isolated settings thanks to Docker containers, so what functions on your computer can function anywhere.

item.title: Surviving Bangkit
item.description: If I had to describe my experience during the Bangkit program in one word, it would be Resilience. Bangkit is not for everyone. I know this sounds intimidating, but trust me - the amount of assignments, meetings, Instructor-Led Training (ILT), and other activities can be pretty overwhelming for people who aren’t used to it. It’s very fast-paced, and most of the time you have to learn by yourself, which I suppose is the point of online learning.

item.title: Who Am I
item.description: Who Am I Hey there! I’m Ezra, a software engineer crafting digital solutions in the vibrant tech landscape of Indonesia. While my days are filled with writing code and solving complex problems, there’s more to my story than just bits and bytes. Think of this website as my digital garden - a space where I cultivate ideas, share experiences, and maybe even help fellow developers along the way. Why create this space?

item.title: Autobiography
item.description: Finding My Path Growing up in Indonesia, I wasn’t the typical “future programmer” you might imagine. Far from being the smartest kid in class, I was actually known as the class clown - a role that came with its share of dismissal from others. But as life would prove, those early academic labels don’t define your future. “七転び八起き” (Nana korobi ya oki) - “Fall seven times, rise eight.” This Japanese proverb has become my life’s motto, embodying my journey from an unlikely beginning to finding my true calling in technology.

item.title: Library
item.description: Welcome to my personal collection of books and resources. If you’re a software engineer, these are must-reads! Designing Data-Intensive Applications An excellent book that covers core concepts in distributed systems, databases, and big data processing. Database Internals A deep dive into database structures and operations, exploring topics like data organization, log-structured databases, and distributed systems. Perfect for expanding on concepts introduced in the first book. 100 Go Mistakes and How to Avoid Them
